Title: OsGATA7 and SMOS1 cooperatively determine rice taste quality by repressing OsGluA2 expression and protein biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major seed storage protein contributing to grain nutritional value but negatively associated with eating and cooking quality/taste
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher OsGluA2 expression increases grain protein content and lowers rice taste value; the elite OsGluA2 haplotype decreases protein content and improves taste value.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsGluA2 was identified as a protein biosynthesis gene directly bound and repressed by SMOS1. 2. OsGluA2 expression was upregulated in osgata7 and smos1 mutants, consistent with increased grain protein content and poorer taste quality. 3. Introgression lines carrying the favorable OsGluA2 haplotype showed reduced protein content and increased taste score.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SMOS1 CUT&Tag; yeast one-hybrid; RT-qPCR in mutants; dual-luciferase repression assay in rice protoplasts; introgression-line phenotyping for protein content and taste value
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in developing grains at 10 DAF as assayed by RT-qPCR context
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: upregulated in 10-DAF grains of osgata7-1 and smos1-1 mutants
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrogen fertilizer context discussed for module function; no direct OsGluA2 treatment experiment reported
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter; reporter pro:OsGluA2 in dual-luciferase assay
Common_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: repressed downstream of SMOS1, which recruits PRC2/LC2 to protein biosynthesis genes such as OsGluA2
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower seed storage protein may improve taste but could reduce nutritional protein content
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favorable OsGluA2 haplotype can be introgressed to lower grain protein and improve taste quality, potentially combined with OsGATA7Hap1 and SMOS1Hap1.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Treated here as a common gene because it is a storage protein/output gene rather than a pathway enzyme or regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Zhonghua11; ShenNong 265; KDML105 haplotype donor in introgression analysis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GATA transcription factor with typical GATA zinc finger domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: protein is a major seed nutrient but elevated levels reduce cooked rice taste quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: OsGATA7 positively improves rice taste quality by lowering grain protein content through activation of SMOS1.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR loss-of-function osgata7 mutants had increased grain protein content and reduced taste value, with altered swelling and textural properties of cooked rice. 2. OsGATA7 directly binds the SMOS1 promoter at a GATC motif and activates SMOS1 transcription. 3. Overexpression and favorable natural haplotype OsGATA7Hap1 were associated with lower protein content and better taste quality.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; complementation; overexpression; haplotype/introgression analysis; RNA-seq; CUT&Tag; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: protein biosynthesis affecting rice eating and cooking quality/taste
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: upstream activator in the OsGATA7-SMOS1-OsGluA2 module controlling grain protein level and taste quality
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: constitutively expressed in all tissues; assayed in developing grains
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrogen fertilizer discussed at module level
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native promoter in complementation; overexpression construct in SN265 background (promoter not explicitly stated)
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Also regulates plant architecture and grain shape; mutants showed altered plant height, tiller number, panicle length, and grain dimensions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: OsGATA7Hap1 and overexpression/activation strategies are promising to reduce grain protein and improve taste, but pleiotropic effects on architecture and grain traits require balancing.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes OsGATA7 as key upstream regulator of taste quality rather than only morphology.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2 domain-containing transcription factor; also known as NGR5
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed storage protein; high levels negatively affect cooked rice taste quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SMOS1 improves rice taste quality by repressing protein biosynthesis genes such as OsGluA2, thereby lowering grain protein content.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. smos1 mutants accumulated more grain protein and had poorer appearance, physicochemical, textural, and taste traits. 2. SMOS1 directly binds the promoters of OsGluA2 and RM1 and represses their transcription. 3. SMOS1 overexpression and the natural haplotype SMOS1Hap1 reduce protein content and improve taste value. 4. OsGATA7 directly activates SMOS1, placing SMOS1 downstream in the regulatory module.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; double-mutant analysis; overexpression; haplotype/introgression analysis; RNA-seq; CUT&Tag; yeast one-hybrid; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression via recruitment of PRC2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses OsGluA2 and RM1; indirectly reduces expression of other protein biosynthesis-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: protein biosynthesis/storage affecting eating and cooking quality
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central downstream regulator directly connecting upstream OsGATA7 to repression of seed storage protein genes and improved taste
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Recruits PRC2 complex; LC2 mentioned as component/partner in assays and model
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: activated by OsGATA7; responds to nitrogen fertilization/nitrate according to prior and current study context
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: recruits PRC2/LC2 complex to repress target gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed in endosperm at 10 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in 10-DAF grain endosperm; Hap1 shows relatively higher expression among natural accessions
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: low, medium, and high nitrogen fertilizer treatments increased protein and reduced taste; SMOS1 is nitrogen responsive
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter for long transcript overexpression; rice ACTIN promoter for short isoform-FLAG overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Both knockout and overexpression increased grain chalkiness traits; also affects plant height, tiller number, panicle length, branch number, and grain size
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: SMOS1Hap1 is an elite allele for improving taste quality; tuning expression or haplotype deployment should consider pleiotropic effects on grain appearance and architecture.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes apparent contradiction between expression of Hap2-4 and protein-content phenotype in diverse germplasm, likely due to complex genetic background.
